Caption: Three electrode half-cell setup used in GITT experiments. The blue circles represent the NVPF active electrode material, and the black circles represent the carbon-based conductive filler. At the particle scale and at the electrode interface, the charge insertion mechanisms are shown. The red and yellow spheres represent sodium cations and electrons, respectively. (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the Web version of this article.)
